Title: Energy Security and Climate Realism Usher in Coal’s Rise
URL: https://wattsupwiththat.com/2026/07/22/energy-security-and-climate-realism-usher-in-coals-rise/
Time Published: 2026-07-22T09:00:00Z
Full Content:
The world's most viewed site on global warming and climate change By Vijay Jayaraj Much of the global energy conversation still pretends that coal is on its way out. The pretense may be comforting to activists, investors, and policymakers who built their plans for a transition to so-called clean energy that never acknowledged the limitations of solar and wind power. However, the newest work by Lars Schernikau and Portia Roberts, The Coal Reality That Western Policy Ignores, shows the opposite: Coal is not being replaced, but rather being layered on top of everything else. The data reveal that global coal consumption is accelerating. The appetite for reliable, baseload power drove usage to an unprecedented high of approximately 9 billion tons in 2025. This volume represents more coal mined and burned than at any time in history, far outstripping levels recorded before the multi-trillion-dollar Western net-zero campaign. In 2025, China alone brought on-line 80 gigawatts of new coal-fired generation – enough to power 60 million households. The Iran war’s disruption to supplies of liquefied natural gas has further boosted demand for coal as nations switched fuels for electricity production. This strategic imperative drove a massive surge in coal shipments across the Pacific. Bangladesh, Japan, the Philippines, South Korea, Taiwan, and Vietnam immediately increased coal imports to avoid blackouts, with China and India leading the shift. While alternative energy installations have expanded, coal utilization has grown right alongside them. The two asset classes are not locked in a zero-sum game but are growing to meet a voracious demand for electricity and industrial production. The world’s proven reserves of coal – deposits that can be extracted economically utilizing current technologies – possess an operational runway of 130 to 150 years at today’s consumption rates. Taking into account remaining geological reserves, Earth holds enough anthracite and bituminous coal for many hundreds of years. Schernikau and Roberts also remind readers that coal sits at the center of food security. Roughly a quarter of the world depends on fertilizers made with coal-derived ammonia. Abandon coal too quickly and you risk not only blackouts but also food production and, ultimately, lives. This return to dependable solid fuels coincides with a long-overdue collapse of the pseudoscientific architecture that sustained for decades the narrative of a fabricated climate crisis. For years, scenarios of extreme weather based on flawed climate models were systematically weaponized by activist bureaucrats to demand the dismantling of a hydrocarbon economy. The most prominent of these fabrications was Representative Concentration Pathway 8.5 (RCP 8.5), alongside its companion RCP 7.0. The international modeling consensus has formally abandoned these deceptions, confirming that RCP 8.5 is officially “implausible.” The apocalyptic scenarios used to terrorize the public and coerce financial markets were never rooted in real-world data. The institutional framework designed to starve conventional energy sectors of investment capital is unraveling before our eyes. The corporate campaign for Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) compliance has had a severe global retrenchment. Global investment consortia, led by institutional giants like BlackRock, have quietly dismantled or significantly downgraded strict climate mandates. International banking groups and global aviation associations have canceled or rolled back aggressive net-zero timelines. The fantasy of a rapid, painless transition away from hydrocarbons has run headfirst into physical laws and geopolitical necessities. Weather-dependent power technologies cannot run steel mills, operate heavy manufacturing, or secure domestic food supplies. The climate establishment can keep writing obituaries for coal, but the market keeps resurrecting it because there is no substitute for its scale, reliability, and availability. Stop pretending that every rise in renewables implies a fall in coal. Stop using extreme scenarios to frighten the public into accepting policy mandates that do not fit the needs of growing economies. And stop telling developing nations that they must sacrifice dependable power in the name of predictive models that no longer command trust. Originally published at PJ Media, July 15th, 2026. Vijay Jayaraj is a Science and Research Associate at the CO2 Coalition, Fairfax, Virginia. He holds an M.S. in environmental sciences from the University of East Anglia and a postgraduate degree in energy management from Robert Gordon University, both in the U.K., and a bachelor’s in engineering from Anna University, India. He served as a research associate with the Changing Oceans Research Unit at University of British Columbia, Canada. Tags: China, coal, ESG, India, Vijay Jayaraj I still remember the dramatic photos of great clouds of “smoke” belching from the “smokestacks” of coal plants. It was all a big lie, and part of the War on Coal. What we were seeing was actually water vapor, but people didn’t know that, or didn’t care. The effect was based on emotion, not logic, and that was by design. And it was always back lit photos of colling towers that were used to show how polluting CO2 is. I took this up with the BBC and the response was they were just using stock photos and had no control over the lighting Dumb excuse, they certainly could have had one of their “journalists” drive to a site and take an honest photo. I had to explain to him that it was actually a nuclear power plant cooling tower.. .. he still didn’t understand. ! It’s funny you should mention this. I took a tour of a very modern coal-fired plant couple of years ago, and the plant manager told me that they don’t schedule tours if at all possible when they know that water vapor will be highly visible condensing out the stack. Pereception is sometimes far more meaningful than a truthful explanation. In my various training courses (mandatory) it is always emphasized that perception is more important than reality. — “It doesn’t matter what is true, it only matters what people believe is true.” – Paul Watson, co-founder of Greenpeace. There is a simple solution to visible condensate from a stack. Use waste heat to reduce relative humidity at the top of the stack to about 90%. The result is a plume that forms like a cloud separated from the stack. It turns something that some regard as ugly into a serenely calming sight – fluffy, almost white cloud some distance from the stack. Cooling towers not so easy but they are not as high or as visible. It was our response to the so-called energy crisis of the 1960s to 1980 or thereabouts. We thought the age of coal was over, with petroleum liquids taking over things like even electricity generation, but then disruptions to global petroleum markets made us realize the enormous coal resources of the U.S. and that these were a low cost resource. I remember in 2005/2006 here in Texas, Governor Rick Perry was about to help usher in 11 (eleven) new coal power plants. See: https://www.houstonpublicmedia.org/articles/news/2006/08/17/3686/txu-to-build-new-plants/ This proposed activity was concomitant with the coming onslaught of the left’s madness against all sensible things, especially coal/CO2. I believe none of the coal plants were ever built. Coal almost single-handedly brought the world to its greatest prosperity. Global population in 1950 was about 2.45 billion; now pushing 8 billion. Coal was integral to that growth. Only wealthier countries are pursuing favorable weather conditions to support electricity generation by wind turbines and solar panels. Shockingly, Pew Research Center shows about 70% of the 8 billion on this planet, or almost 6 billion, are living on less than $10/day and have yet to join the industrial revolution! For those is poorer developing countries, using abundant coal for the generation of continuous and uninterruptable electricity is best for their country’s’ economic development. “The world’s proven reserves of coal – deposits that can be extracted economically utilizing current technologies – possess an operational runway of 130 to 150 years at today’s consumption rates.” This is the issue that is often overlooked by the AGW skeptics. Whilst I am also an AGW skeptic, I try to look at the broader picture of future energy use. As Ronald Stein mentioned in his post above, “Shockingly, Pew Research Center shows about 70% of the 8 billion on this planet, or almost 6 billion, are living on less than $10/day and have yet to join the industrial revolution!” If one is concerned about the future prosperity and wellbeing of the world population, then we should plan for an energy consumption which is far, far greater than today’s consumption rate. Relying only on fossil fuels would result in a future catastrophe resulting from energy costs which would be much higher than today’s costs, because of the increasing scarity of fossil fuels in far less than 150 years, assuming world poverty is addressed. This is why we should begin the development of alternative and renewable energy sources right now, because it is clear that such development is a long process, involving lots of inefficiences and hazards that have to be addressed. I consider the current ‘net zero’ policy of mandates and subsidies merely a stimulant to encourage research and experimentation into alternative sources of energy. What doesn’t work efficiently will (or at least should) eventually be discarded. You are right that any energy transition is going to take a long time-many decades and include all forms of energy generation that should compete naturally without subsidies. Presently, coal at scale with modern HELE plants is the cheapest generator of base load electricity, gas is great for backup as is hydro, whilst nuclear is gaining ground as a safe reliable long-term producer. Wind and solar are increasingly efficient, but are less productive and unreliable than fossil fuels, and need critical backup, that can’t presently be provided using batteries for more than a few hours- certainly not days!. Essentially, we need better technology and further costly research if we want to replace fossil fuels. Thus, Net Zero is a loser from all reasonable perspectives and need to be cancelled ASAP before it de-industrialises economies and prevents the ability of nations to undertake required energy research and have the industrial capacity to make new technology. “Wind and solar are increasingly efficient, but are less productive and unreliable than fossil fuels, and need critical backup, that can’t presently be provided using batteries for more than a few hours- certainly not days!.” I’m not in favour of using large tracts of land for windmills and solar panels, and the additional requirement for long-distance power lines. However, the roofs of buildings are essentially free spaces. Imagine a new home designed to maximize the the production of solar energy by integrating the construction of the house with the installation of solar tiles or solar panels which cover the entire roof area, apart from the solar hot water heater. An average all-electric Australian home, with a solar hot water heater, consumes between 11 and 15 kWh of electricity per day. 60KWh of sodium-ion battery storage would provide 4-5 full days of power supply for the home, when the sun doesn’t shine. A bit more than a few hours! Notice how Vijay’s posts get ever more assertive as we “deniers” take the “wind ” out of the thermometer-readers’ “sales”. (Sorry, couldn’t resist.) I would compare the climate alarmist cult with a skunk, even after they leave you still have to deal with the stench. Title: NVIDIA Agent Toolkit が新たな Omniverse ライブラリで拡張され、AI エージェントによるシミュレーション対応のワールド構築が可能に
URL: https://prtimes.jp/main/html/rd/p/000000637.000012662.html
Time Published: 2026-07-22T07:40:49Z
Full Content:
PR TIMESのご利用について 資料をダウンロード NVIDIA ニュース概要： NVIDIA Agent Toolkit に NVIDIA Omniverse ライブラリが新たに追加され、AI エージェントは、ソフトウェア開発者が既存のアプリケーションにフィジカル AI 機能を統合できるよう支援 NVIDIA RTX センサー シミュレーション、GPU アクセラレーションによる物理シミュレーション、およびシミュレーション対応アセットの検証を行うための新しい Omniverse ライブラリが、GitHub で公開 SideFX と PTC は、フィジカル AI 向けの 3D アプリケーションに Omniverse ライブラリを統合。これらは、NVIDIA RTX Spark から NVIDIA DGX Station に至るまで、クラウドおよびローカルの AI システムをサポート Blender に Omniverse ライブラリを統合するための新しい NVIDIA ブループリントが公開 NVIDIA は本日、NVIDIA Agent Toolkit に NVIDIA Omniverse™ ライブラリが組み込まれたことを発表しました。これは、既存のアプリケーションにフィジカル AI 機能を付加し、シミュレーション用の 3D コンテンツを準備するために必要なツールやスキルを AI エージェントに提供する、ソフトウェア コンポーネントのコレクションです。 ロボットや工場、自律型システムは、現実世界で稼働する前に、シミュレーション環境で設計、テスト、トレーニングを行う必要があります。シミュレーション用の 3D コンテンツを準備するには、単に見た目がリアルであるだけでは不十分です。アセットには、適切な構造、マテリアル、スケール、ラベル、センサー、そして物理特性が備わっている必要があります。NVIDIA Agent Toolkit に NVIDIA Omniverse ライブラリが統合されたことで、AI エージェントはワークフローの構築、シーンの検証、問題の指摘、アセットの準備を行うためのツールやスキルを備えることになります。これにより、開発者は 3D コンテンツからシミュレーション可能な環境への移行をより迅速に進められるようになります。 NVIDIA の創業者/CEO であるジェンスン フアン (Jensen Huang) は次のように述べています。「フィジカル AI の時代は、まずシミュレーションの中で築かれるでしょう。Omniverse ライブラリを組み込んだ NVIDIA Agent Toolkit は、開発者がすでに使用している 3D ツールに AI エージェントを導入します。これにより、ロボットや工場、自律型システムが現実世界に投入されるはるか前に、それらをトレーニングしテストするための、シミュレーション対応の環境構築が可能になるのです」 SideFX や PTC といったソフトウェア メーカーは、エージェント対応のセンサー シミュレーション、物理演算、アセット検証を実現するために Omniverse ライブラリを統合しています。これにより、開発者やテクニカル アーティストが 3D コンテンツ制作ですでに使用しているアプリケーションやワークフローに、自律的に動作するエージェント型 AI を組み込むことが可能になります。 Omniverse ライブラリが NVIDIA Agent Toolkit にフィジカル AI の機能を提供 NVIDIA Agent Toolkit は、ツール、スキル、データソースを連携させる AI エージェントの構築を行うソフトウェア メーカーを支援します。Omniverse ライブラリは、既存のアプリケーション内で呼び出し可能なセンサー シミュレーション、GPU アクセラレーションによる物理演算、シミュレーション対応アセットの検証といった機能を提供し、それらのエージェントの能力を 3D およびフィジカル AI のワークフローへと拡張します。 ovrtx、ovphysx、CAD-to-SimReady スキルなど、新しい Omniverse ライブラリが GitHub で公開されました。これにより、AI エージェントは、シーンの検証、変更のテスト、シミュレーション用 3D アセットの準備といったワークフローを構築するためのツールを利用できるようになります。また、Blender に Omniverse ライブラリを統合するための新しいブループリントも GitHub で利用可能になりました。 これらのライブラリの主な機能は以下の通りです。 NVIDIA RTX センサー シミュレーション: ovrtx は、アプリケーションが 3D シーンからカメラ、LiDRA、レーダー、その他のセンサー出力を生成するのに役立ちます。そのため、開発者と AI エージェントは、フィジカル AI システムが仮想環境をどのように認識するかをテストできます。 物理的動作: ovphysx は、GPU で高速化された物理学を使用して、衝突、質量、摩擦、運動などの特性を用いて 3D シーンに現実的な挙動をもたらすため、チームは最初にオブジェクトとシステムがシミュレーションでどのように相互作用するかをテストできます。 シミュレーション対応 3D オブジェクト: CAD-to-SimReady スキルは、コンピューター支援設計 (CAD) データを OpenUSD 上に構築された SimReady アセットに変換するのに役立ち、フィジカルAI シミュレーションと仮想テストに必要な特性を 3D コンテンツに与えます。 ソフトウェア開発企業が Omniverse ライブラリを活用した開発に着手 SideFX や PTC といったソフトウェア メーカーに加え、ForgeCAD、Lightwheel、Moonlake AI、Palatial などのスタートアップ企業が NVIDIA Agent Toolkit の一部となった Omniverse ライブラリをいち早く採用し、開発に活用し始めています。 SideFX は、OpenUSD ワークフローや ovrtx、ovphysx ライブラリを使用し、エージェントが同社の「Houdini」によるプロシージャル 3D コンテンツ制作ワークフローに Omniverse ライブラリを統合する上でどのような役割を果たせるかを模索しています。これにより、テクニカル アーティストは、コンテンツの生成、物理挙動のテスト、そしてシミュレーションに向けた準備を行うための新たな手段を得ることになります。 SideFX の社長兼 CEO である Kim Davidson 氏は次のように話しています。「シミュレーション、ロボティクス、産業用 AI に求められる、複雑かつ制御可能な世界を構築するには、プロシージャル 3D 制作が不可欠です。NVIDIA Omniverse ライブラリと OpenUSD を活用することで、SideFX はエージェント対応ツールが Houdini のワークフローをどのように支援できるかを検証しています。これにより、テクニカル アーティストはクリエイティブなプロセスを自らコントロールしつつ、シミュレーション用のプロシージャル コンテンツのレビュー、テスト、準備を行えるようになります」 PTC Onshape CAD および製品データ管理 (PDM) プラットフォームは、OpenUSD と ovrtx を活用して、クラウドネイティブな設計ワークフローと物理シミュレーションを連携させています。これにより、製品設計データと、CAD、PDM、コラボレーション、シミュレーションといった各ワークフローとの一貫性が保たれるよう支援しています。 PTC の社長兼 CEO である Neil Barua 氏は次のように話しています。「エンジニアリング チームは、開発プロセス全体を通じて、設計、コラボレーション、シミュレーションをより緊密に連携させる方法を求めています。PTC と NVIDIA の取り組みはこの広範なビジョンを支えるものであり、NVIDIA Omniverse ライブラリは、検証やテストを製品設計の現場により近い段階で実施可能にするシミュレーション対応ワークフローの実現に貢献しています」 SIGGRAPH で展示された「SimReady」Blender は、NVIDIA Omniverse ライブラリと NVIDIA NemoClaw™ を使用して Blender 上で構築されたサンプル ワークフローです。これは、クリエイターが制御を維持したまま、NVIDIA RTX センサー シミュレーション、物理演算、検証機能といったエージェント対応のシミュレーション機能を、既存の 3D アプリケーションにどのように組み込めるかを示すものです。現在、このワークフローは、Blender への Omniverse ライブラリ統合に向けたブループリントとして公開されています。 また、このデモでは、NVIDIA Agent Toolkit の一部である Omniverse ライブラリを使用して構築されたこれらのワークフローが、NVIDIA RTX Spark™ を搭載したコンパクトな RTX システムから、NVIDIA GB300 搭載の NVIDIA DGX Station™ に至るまで、どのようにローカル環境で実行できるかを紹介しています。RTX Spark システムは、今秋より ASUS、Dell Technologies、HP、Lenovo、Microsoft Surface、MSI から発売され、その後 Acer や GIGABYTE からもモデルが投入される予定です。DGX Station システムは、ASUS、Dell、GIGABYTE、HP、MSI、Supermicro、Exxact から注文可能です。 NVIDIA Inception プログラムに参加している企業を含むスタートアップ各社も、Omniverse のライブラリやスキルを活用し、エージェントが支援するアセットおよびシーン準備のワークフローを導入しています。Palatial は、Omniverse の CAD-to-SimReady スキルを使用し、CAD データから SimReady アセットの作成と検証を大規模に自動化しています。Lightwheel は、OpenUSD を基盤とする Omniverse Content Agents を同社の SimReadyGen 技術に活用し、テキスト プロンプトから物理的に正確な SimReady アセットを生成しています。 ForgeCAD や Moonlake AI は、Omniverse の機能を活用したエージェント駆動型の 3D コンテンツ ワークフローを模索しており、フィジカル AI シミュレーション用のアセット生成、拡張、準備の効率化に取り組んでいます。 SIGGRAPH での NVIDIA 基調講演をぜひご覧ください。NVIDIA Omniverse ライブラリの詳細や、利用可能なサンプルおよびドキュメントについてもご確認ください。 ※本発表資料は米国時間 2026 年 7 月 20 日に発表されたプレスリリースの抄訳です。 NVIDIA について NVIDIA (NASDAQ: NVDA) はAIとアクセラレーテッド コンピューティングの世界的なリーダーです。 Title: Book Review: The Toll Of Fortune
URL: https://www.americanthinker.com/articles/2026/07/book-review-the-toll-of-fortune/
Time Published: 2026-07-22T04:00:00Z
Full Content:
A literary masterpiece that mirrors much of society today. Twilight Patriot | July 22, 2026 For a little more than a year, I have been in the habit of reviewing works of fiction by right-leaning authors who are sympathetic to the worldview of Twilight Patriot. It was my friend Alexander Macris, from Contemplations on the Tree of Woe, who helped me get this started: Alex has always admired right-wingers who push back on left-wing cultural dominance by creating art of their own. Today’s entry is The Toll of Fortune: An Indo-European Origin Saga, by A. J. R. Klopp, who was kind enough to send me a free copy last month. The Toll of Fortune is a hard book to put into a category. Is it historical fiction? Not really. It’s set in 3309 BC, well before the invention of writing, and the cultures that appear in it are known only through archaeology and comparative linguistics. Is it fantasy? Sort of: Klopp’s characters believe in a lot of things that we would call supernatural, but they don’t live in an invented world with its own languages and races and geography — Klopp is writing about our actual ancestors. The author of The Toll of Fortune is a man of diverse interests and experiences. He studied physics and astronomy in college, then spent a few years doing radio astronomy near the summit of Mauna Kea, where he studied star formation rates in distant galaxies. Then he decided to move on to something more human, and went to law school. After three years practicing corporate law (he specialized in acquisitions, mergers, and debt financing) he ended up working as a bond trader for BlackRock. But now, he’s finally found the time for his “real passion,” which “is history, especially retracing the steps of our ancestors to the remote past.” The Toll of Fortune is an adventure story in the mould of the great epics, and the main action begins when a group of Proto-Indo-European youths (the Danger-Runner band, away from home on the rite-of-passage that will make them into men) are massacred by primeval monsters long thought to be extinct. (Neanderthals, perhaps? Here is Alexander Macris’s article about the possibility that Homo neanderthalensis used to eat H. sapiens.) After a few survivors limp back to the Great Tent, bearing horrid wounds, the Clan-Father Ever-Wheel gathers a “special warband, whose purpose will be to avenge the Danger-Runner band and end the race of whichever miscreants committed this heinous act!” Thus begins our adventure – but it’s not just a battle between the heroes and the fallen elder race that they sometimes call “the Titans” or “the Gods’ First Born Children.” This warband will also encounter a strange society ruled from the Broad-Eave Hall, a literal longhouse whose Kin-Lord and Maven preside over a way of life very different from their own. And it is through the foil of the Maven’s people that the reader is able to see Ever-Wheel’s people as the heroes, even though (like any group of copper-age warriors) they do a lot of things that seem barbaric to us. Barbarism is a good thing, Klopp seems to be saying, when you compare it to civilization gone wrong. The heroes of The Toll of Fortune are Proto-Indo-Europeans, the speakers of a language that’s ancestral to the mother tongues of about half the people in the world today. All of the Germanic languages (including English), plus Spanish and French and the other Romance languages, along with Greek, Russian, Iranian, and most of the languages of India, are all descended from a single language that was spoken on the Pontic Steppe, in what is now Ukraine and southern Russia, about five thousand years ago. The people who spoke it were cattle-herders, and they were probably also the first people to ever ride horses. Needless to say, this was a skill that served them well in their various wars of conquest, as they spread far and wide at the expense of less adaptive civilizations. In a way, it’s odd that most people know so little about the Proto-Indo-Europeans, since anyone with Caucasian ancestry is, in large part, their descendant. And they also passed down bits and pieces of their worldview to us. Do you pray to a deity called Father-in-Heaven? So did they. Do you eat cattle, pigs, and chickens with gusto, while recoiling from dog meat? So did they. Most ancient cultures did not have those quirks. Klopp’s thesis is that we should know and care more about our ancestors. And he tries to draw their society as accurately as he can, based on the latest discoveries in archeology and paleo genomics. The characters that he creates are strange, and yet in some ways familiar. They don’t live by Christian ethics (no one did, three millennia before Christ) but despite their raiding and reaving of the “Farm People” whom they look down upon, they are shown, over and over again, to be more honorable, and more like us, than the various enemies they have to fight. It’s the mark of a non-woke mind that one can honor his ancestors for what they did right, regardless of the big differences in custom and morality between their times and ours. And that shouldn’t stop with the American Founders, or the Pilgrims on the Mayflower, or the Crusader kings. Before any of those people, at the very headwaters of what became Western Civilization, are the Proto-Indo-Europeans. A hunter named Wolf and his son Bear are the central characters of The Toll of Fortune, and they both make it all the way to the final battle with the Titans. But Wolf isn’t quite a member of the tribe in which he lives — he married into it, and he’s always been somewhat of an outsider, someone who’s not expected to ever join the Shouting Council that advises the Clan Fathe But the other men respect Wolf for what he is – a man who’s brave, quick-thinking, skilled in combat, and full of practical knowledge about the beasts and monsters that never stop menacing mankind. When we first meet him, he’s saving a vassal tribe from being annihilated by “wolves in dogs’ skins,” vile creatures who can give men what we would call rabies, but what Wolf and his 16-year-old son Bear call the morbus bite. Again and again, we are introduced to the Indo-European worldview through conversations between fathers and sons, like this one: Bear: ‘Who is Chaos, Father?’ Wolf: ‘Chaos takes on many forms, boy. But Chaos is not a god like the Sky-Father or the Lord of Storms – Chaos was here long before the gods and will remain even after they are gone and the sky crashes down upon us, and mountains are blown to dust by the Krivach.’ Bear digested these words slowly. He’d never heard of sacrifices to any god who came before the Sky-Father, certainly not this ‘chaos’ fellow. Wolf: ‘Chaos is the yawning chasm that existed when even time itself had a dawn. Chaos is the Ever-Serpent, coiling itself about the roots of the Axle-Tree at the base of the World, impelling its growth and causing its rot. Chaos knows all and yet knows nothing. It grants no intercession on account of offerings or sacrifice. So do not try! But that does not mean Chaos will never present you with gifts, either. These gifts will manifest in ways that can only be seen by the keenest eye.’ Later on: Wolf: ‘Long before Rain and your mother’s people brought cattle to these plains, they too were hunters. The same as my own forefathers. And before them an Age of Ice held sway – some say since the beginning of time, for the Sun – that your mother’s people call the Sky-Father – had yet to appear. During that time, beasts with fangs the size of my arm preyed on our ancestors in the darkness. In those days only the Lord of Storms, called Perqueunos, ruled the sky. And only when he defeated the Great Serpent, could we flourish.’ And then there is this: Bear: ‘Why do the Farm People make slaves?’ Wolf: ‘Because they can. Because they are weak and because those they take as slaves are weaker still.’ Bear: ‘Shouldn’t we take slaves too then?’ Wolf: ‘Strong men do not need slaves. Resourceful men do not want slaves. And cunning men have no use for an enslaved mind….’ Bear: ‘But we could if we desired it?...’ It makes one think of the discourse about slavery during the American Founding, when a lot of people agreed that slavery was bad for society, but they didn’t always agree about why, and they weren’t passionate enough to actually put a stop to it. But if the Proto-Indo-Europeans were ambivalent about how to treat people weaker than themselves, there was no equivocating about the other side of the issue. They themselves must never be weak. Indeed, Klopp is really talking to us moderns when he has the old Clan-Father say: ‘These lands are not our lands. Not yet. They belonged to others who have left them to waste. But they will be your lands, my son, if you can stay here long enough... But beware. One day, someone might lurk over the horizon. Someone who waits until you are weakened, and leaving the land to waste, and then pushes you out.’ What actually happens in The Toll of Fortune? Well, a lot of the same things that you’ll see in any good adventure novel. There are battles, long journeys into strange lands, and scenes inspired by Homer, Shakespeare, and the Beowulf poet. There are great warriors and great smiths. There is a man who takes a meteorite (the only source of iron that existed back then) and forges it into the Hammer of Heaven. The Toll of Fortune is the tale of a boy becoming a man, and then becoming more than a man. And it is a tale about men who must fight the monsters of primeval chaos, and then turn around and fight a new kind of monster, the society of the Broad-Eave Hall, whose drugged-out, faux-matriarchal rulers are there to show that a civilization that’s too confident in its own knowledge, and too controlling of its people, can be worse than barbarism. In case you’re interested in reading more, I have a longer and more detailed review of The Toll of Fortune at the Twilight Patriot Substack. And if you don’t need any more convincing, then you can go straight ahead and purchase Klopp’s masterpiece here. Twilight Patriot is the pen name for a young American who lives in South Carolina, where he is currently working toward a graduate degree. Image: Book cover. Now more than ever, the ability to speak our minds is crucial to the republic we cherish. If what you see on American Thinker resonates with you, please consider supporting our work with a donation of as much or as little as you can give. Every dollar contributed helps us pay our staff and keep our ideas heard and our voices strong. Thank you.
--------------------------------------------------

Title: NUPRC awards 37 oil blocks, warns against delays
URL: https://punchng.com/nuprc-awards-37-oil-blocks-warns-against-delays/
Time Published: 2026-07-22T01:50:02Z
Full Content:
Most Widely Read Newspaper Kindly share this story: The Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commission on Tuesday declared 31 companies as winners of 37 oil and gas blocks under the 2025 Licensing Round, despite what the commission described as sustained threats and pressure mounted against members of its evaluation team before the conclusion of the exercise. The successful conclusion of the commercial bid conference marked the end of an eight-month licensing process, with the winning firms now required to pay their signature bonuses and satisfy other post-award conditions within 90 days or risk forfeiting the assets to reserve bidders. Speaking after the commercial bid conference in Abuja, the Commission Chief Executive of the NUPRC, Oritsemeyiwa Eyesan, disclosed that officials involved in evaluating the bids faced repeated intimidation throughout the process but refused to compromise the integrity of the exercise. She said the threats persisted until the eve of the commercial bid opening. Eyesan said, “It has been a journey… If you have been told anything contrary to the fact that this process was going to be credible and transparent, do not believe it.” Commending members of the evaluation committee, she added, “The evaluators have worked tirelessly since June 12. They have been inundated with calls and with threats, serious threats, but they stood their ground. Up until yesterday, we were still threatened, but we stood our ground to say that the times have changed. Nigeria is really open for business.” She said President Bola Tinubu had mandated the commission to ensure a credible process and thanked the evaluators and observers from the Nigeria Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative for supporting the exercise. The commission announced that 31 companies emerged successful after 143 companies submitted about 200 bids for 37 oil and gas blocks out of the 50 assets offered during the licensing round. The successful companies include SSonic Petroleum Limited, CFP Pipeline and Flowlines, Dutchford E&P Limited, Attabanson Global Company Limited, Rosem Energy Limited, Pivot-GIS Limited, Network E&P, Asharami, LexOil, BVOF, Gupsco Energy Limited, Saratoga, Volante, Concept-Reel Petroleum Services Limited, Clinton Oil Field, Nuway Oaklane Limited, Ramec Italia. Others are Blueridge E&P, Up Energies Limited, AYM Shafa, Blackrock Holdings Limited, Funtay Integrated Business Limited, Riparian Development and Production Limited, Nikstallis, Stardeep Petroleum, Dakoda & U Limited, Southborne Oil and Gas Limited, Lanaka Petroleum, Highban Resources Limited and Eyre Energy Limited. The commission explained that the successful companies had emerged only as preferred bidders and would receive Petroleum Prospecting Licences after meeting all statutory conditions under the Petroleum Industry Act. Related News BREAKING: Tinubu approves expansion of Nigerian Army to 12 divisions No more hostilities, Akpabio apologises as Oshiomhole recalls ‘massaging woman’s legs’ jibe Army declares soldier wanted for selling military uniforms to terrorists Eyesan urged the winners to immediately commence the post-award process. She said, “These firms will only be presented final awards after the payment of the appropriate signature bonus and the approval of the Minister of Petroleum Resources in line with the Petroleum Industry Act, 2021.” She warned that failure to fulfil the post-award conditions within 90 days would invalidate the awards, allowing the commission to invite reserve bidders. The commission explained that the commercial bid process was designed to eliminate human interference through an automated weighted scoring system. Officials said technical evaluations had been completed before the commercial bids were opened publicly, while no one, including members of the evaluation team, had prior access to the commercial bids. “The weighted score is 40 per cent. All these things are automated. The computer calculates everything. Nobody is using a pen to write any figures. This demonstrates the transparent, efficient and robust process built into this licensing round,” the commission stated. Meanwhile, the Minister of State for Petroleum Resources (Oil), Senator Heineken Lokpobiri, said the Petroleum Industry Act had ended the discretionary allocation of oil blocks in Nigeria. “The PIA, unfortunately for some people, has prevented discretionary allocation of oil blocks,” he said jokingly. He stressed that the law guarantees fairness and credibility, assuring investors that no one knows the content of commercial bids before they are officially opened. Lokpobiri also warned successful bidders against treating licences as speculative assets. “In the past, I have seen people go round conferences across the world carrying licences and looking for partners who never came. Those days must be over. The licences issued today must translate into actual field development and production,” he said. Also speaking, the Minister of State for Petroleum Resources (Gas), Ekperikpe Ekpo, said the licensing round reflected the Federal Government’s commitment to transparency, competitiveness and credibility. “The Federal Government remains firmly committed to creating an enabling environment that attracts investment, accelerates exploration and production, and unlocks the full value of Nigeria’s hydrocarbon resources,” Ekpo said. The successful conclusion of the commercial bid conference marked the end of an eight-month licensing process, with the winning firms now required to pay their signature bonuses and satisfy other post-award conditions within 90 days or risk forfeiting the assets to reserve bidders. Speaking after the commercial bid conference in Abuja, the Commission Chief Executive of the NUPRC, Oritsemeyiwa Eyesan, disclosed that officials involved in evaluating the bids faced repeated intimidation throughout the process but refused to compromise the integrity of the exercise. She said the threats persisted until the eve of the commercial bid opening. Eyesan said, “It has been a journey… If you have been told anything contrary to the fact that this process was going to be credible and transparent, do not believe it.” Commending members of the evaluation committee, she added, “The evaluators have worked tirelessly since June 12. They have been inundated with calls and with threats, serious threats, but they stood their ground. Up until yesterday, we were still threatened, but we stood our ground to say that the times have changed. Nigeria is really open for business.” She said President Bola Tinubu had mandated the commission to ensure a credible process and thanked the evaluators and observers from the Nigeria Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative for supporting the exercise. The commission announced that 31 companies emerged successful after 143 companies submitted about 200 bids for 37 oil and gas blocks out of the 50 assets offered during the licensing round. The successful companies include SSonic Petroleum Limited, CFP Pipeline and Flowlines, Dutchford E&P Limited, Attabanson Global Company Limited, Rosem Energy Limited, Pivot-GIS Limited, Network E&P, Asharami, LexOil, BVOF, Gupsco Energy Limited, Saratoga, Volante, Concept-Reel Petroleum Services Limited, Clinton Oil Field, Nuway Oaklane Limited, Ramec Italia. Others are Blueridge E&P, Up Energies Limited, AYM Shafa, Blackrock Holdings Limited, Funtay Integrated Business Limited, Riparian Development and Production Limited, Nikstallis, Stardeep Petroleum, Dakoda & U Limited, Southborne Oil and Gas Limited, Lanaka Petroleum, Highban Resources Limited and Eyre Energy Limited. The commission explained that the successful companies had emerged only as preferred bidders and would receive Petroleum Prospecting Licences after meeting all statutory conditions under the Petroleum Industry Act. The commission explained that the commercial bid process was designed to eliminate human interference through an automated weighted scoring system. Officials said technical evaluations had been completed before the commercial bids were opened publicly, while no one, including members of the evaluation team, had prior access to the commercial bids. “The weighted score is 40 per cent. All these things are automated. The computer calculates everything. Nobody is using a pen to write any figures. This demonstrates the transparent, efficient and robust process built into this licensing round,” the commission stated. Meanwhile, the Minister of State for Petroleum Resources (Oil), Senator Heineken Lokpobiri, said the Petroleum Industry Act had ended the discretionary allocation of oil blocks in Nigeria. “The PIA, unfortunately for some people, has prevented discretionary allocation of oil blocks,” he said jokingly. He stressed that the law guarantees fairness and credibility, assuring investors that no one knows the content of commercial bids before they are officially opened. Lokpobiri also warned successful bidders against treating licences as speculative assets. “In the past, I have seen people go round conferences across the world carrying licences and looking for partners who never came. Those days must be over. The licences issued today must translate into actual field development and production,” he said. Also speaking, the Minister of State for Petroleum Resources (Gas), Ekperikpe Ekpo, said the licensing round reflected the Federal Government’s commitment to transparency, competitiveness and credibility. “The Federal Government remains firmly committed to creating an enabling environment that attracts investment, accelerates exploration and production, and unlocks the full value of Nigeria’s hydrocarbon resources,” Ekpo said. She warned that failure to fulfil the post-award conditions within 90 days would invalidate the awards, allowing the commission to invite reserve bidders. The commission explained that the commercial bid process was designed to eliminate human interference through an automated weighted scoring system. Officials said technical evaluations had been completed before the commercial bids were opened publicly, while no one, including members of the evaluation team, had prior access to the commercial bids. “The weighted score is 40 per cent. All these things are automated. The computer calculates everything. Nobody is using a pen to write any figures. This demonstrates the transparent, efficient and robust process built into this licensing round,” the commission stated. Meanwhile, the Minister of State for Petroleum Resources (Oil), Senator Heineken Lokpobiri, said the Petroleum Industry Act had ended the discretionary allocation of oil blocks in Nigeria. “The PIA, unfortunately for some people, has prevented discretionary allocation of oil blocks,” he said jokingly. He stressed that the law guarantees fairness and credibility, assuring investors that no one knows the content of commercial bids before they are officially opened. Those days must be over. The licences issued today must translate into actual field development and production,” he said. Title: BlackRock CEO Bullish on Bitcoin as Leverage Cleanup Improves Crypto Structure
URL: https://zycrypto.com/blackrock-ceo-bullish-on-bitcoin-as-leverage-cleanup-improves-crypto-structure/
Time Published: 2026-07-21T22:08:26Z
Full Content:
BlackRock CEO Larry Fink expressed renewed optimism about financial markets over the coming year, while also offering a more constructive assessment of Bitcoin, saying the cryptocurrency market has become more stable following the removal of excessive leverage. Speaking in a CNBC interview, Fink said, “I’m very bullish on the markets over the next 12 months,” citing the ongoing technological transformation driven by artificial intelligence and infrastructure investment. Although his bullish outlook focused on the broader market, Fink also reflected on Bitcoin’s evolution, contrasting today’s environment with previous crypto market cycles. Additionally, discussing financial stability, Fink noted that one of his biggest concerns in earlier years had been the amount of leverage built into cryptocurrency markets. “I was always worried about the leverage in Bitcoin and crypto. There was too much leveraged players in it. That’s why we had to wash out. And I think there’s more stability at these levels here,” he said. Notably, his remarks suggest that the painful deleveraging experienced across the digital asset sector has helped create a healthier market structure. Rather than viewing leverage as a major systemic threat today, Fink said he sees far less implicit leverage across global capital markets than during the period surrounding the 2008 financial crisis. The comments add to the growing shift in institutional sentiment toward Bitcoin, with many large asset managers increasingly treating the cryptocurrency as part of a diversified investment portfolio rather than a purely speculative asset. Beyond cryptocurrencies, Fink spent much of the discussion highlighting artificial intelligence as the next major driver of economic growth. According to the CEO, demand for computing infrastructure continues to exceed available supply, creating investment opportunities across data centers, semiconductor manufacturing, and power generation. “We see as a big investor in data centers, the demand for compute is not slowing down. It’s growing faster,” Fink stated. He warned that insufficient investment in electricity grids and power generation could become a bottleneck for AI adoption, stressing that affordable and reliable energy will determine which regions benefit most from the technology boom. Meanwhile, Fink also voiced concern that smaller businesses could struggle to compete if computing costs remain elevated, saying the industry must continue reducing the cost of AI infrastructure. He pointed to the firm’s expanding use of automation and artificial intelligence to process more transactions without increasing headcount, while AI-assisted software development has accelerated coding efficiency. The executive said these technological gains have helped expand BlackRock’s operating margins and should continue supporting profitability as private markets and retirement-related businesses grow. At the time of writing, BTC changed hands at $66,242, reflecting a 2% gain over the past 24 hours.
--------------------------------------------------

Title: Jim Cramer Says Stop Betting on Volatile Tech Stocks and Buy These “Boring” Sectors Instead
URL: https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/07/21/jim-cramer-says-stop-betting-on-volatile-tech-stocks-and-buy-these-boring-sectors-instead/
Time Published: 2026-07-21T22:04:33Z
Full Content:
Investing JPMorgan crushed Q2 with $7.70 EPS versus $5.80 expected and authorized a $50 billion buyback; J.B. Hunt has surged 97% over the past year. Microsoft fell 20% over the past year despite a $37 billion AI run rate, while Salesforce dropped 34% year to date with Cramer holding no conviction below $160. Cramer is fleeing tech as the VIX jumps 25% in a week and chip volatility hits a 30-year high versus the broader market. Act now: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 AI stocks — and JPMorgan Chase didn't make the cut. Grab the names FREE today. Sending You to Google News in 3 © Spencer Platt / Getty Images Jim Cramer used his Tuesday, July 20, CNBC Squawk on the Street appearance to lay out one of his most emphatic sector rotation calls of the year: move away from semiconductors, software, and AI-linked mega-caps, and toward banks, trucking, and steadier cash-flow industrials. The 10-year Treasury sits at 4.55%, the VIX at 18.77 has jumped 24.9% in a week, and Cramer says chip volatility is at a 30-year high versus the market. Cramer framed the daily experience of owning tech vividly: “Every day when you come in, when you’re with tech… you’re basically tied up in front of a freight train on the tracks, and someone cuts it just before you die. And I don’t want that. I would rather be in JP Morgan.“ He added, “With the exception of Apple, I fully expect at the end of the day to be down. Maybe today’s the day where I make some money in Micron. But right now… can I please be in a trucking company that’s about to report?” JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM | JPM Price Prediction) posted Q2 2026 EPS of $7.70 versus a $5.80 estimate, revenue of $57.35 billion, and ROTCE of 23%. Equity Markets revenue jumped 86% year over year to $6.03 billion, and the board authorized a fresh $50 billion buyback. Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) reported similarly strong results on July 14. JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon said the U.S. economy has “demonstrated notable resiliency this year, with stronger business investment and hiring,” aided by AI-driven capital investment and fiscal stimulus. Shares trade at a trailing P/E of 15 with an analyst price target of $367.45, indicating analysts see a sliver of upside from the stock’s $345.23 share price at the market’s close on Tuesday. J.B. Hunt Transport Services (NASDAQ:JBHT) delivered Q2 EPS of $1.91 versus $1.73 expected on revenue of $3.50 billion, up 19.4%. Intermodal revenue rose 22% to $1.75 billion with operating income up 58%. These are terrific results, and similar strength might be coming for the rest of the sector. The stock is up 50.11% year to date and 97.05% over the past year, making it a low-drama compounding profile that might be worth a look today. Corning (NYSE:GLW) crystallized Cramer’s frustration. “I owned Corning for the trust. It went up 100 [basis] points over a period of a month. I was a genius then. It lost 100 points in two days. I’m an idiot,” he said. Shares are down 21.45% over the past month, yet still up 186.6% over the past year. Optical Communications revenue rose 36% year over year to $1.85 billion on AI data center demand, and CEO Wendell Weeks said Corning “finalized two more hyperscaler deals similar in size and duration to our recently announced multiyear, up-to-$6 billion agreement with Meta.” Cramer believes extreme volatility has made technology stocks tough to own right now. Today, he prefers banks and trucking companies such as JPMorgan and J.B. Hunt, which offer strong earnings growth and steadier cash flow. The broader-market rotation could continue if AI spending slows and investors keep moving away from speculative technology stocks. Contact [email protected] for any questions or corrections. Title: Netflix “Is Not a Broken Company” and Trades At Just 19x Earnings. Jim Cramer Says Start Buying
URL: https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/07/21/netflix-is-not-a-broken-company-and-trades-at-just-19x-earnings-jim-cramer-says-start-buying/
Time Published: 2026-07-21T21:43:38Z
Full Content:
Investing Cramer called NFLX a buying opportunity at 19x forward earnings after shares dropped 44% over the past year to close at $67.60. Netflix fell nearly 8% within an hour of its Q2 earnings filing while SPY and QQQ both rose. Netflix completed a record $4.7 billion buyback in Q2, with $27 billion in remaining authorization and ad revenue targeting $3 billion this year. Act now: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 AI stocks — and Netflix didn't make the cut. Grab the names FREE today. Sending You to Google News in 3 © 24/7 Wall St. Jim Cramer used his Tuesday, July 20, CNBC Mad Money segment to defend Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX | NFLX Price Prediction) after a punishing post-earnings sell-off, telling viewers the streamer’s slide into the high-$60s makes the stock worthy of a closer look. “This is not a broken company. It’s one of the best companies around with one of the best products, and the numbers are still better than most,” Cramer said, framing the stock’s 19x forward earnings multiple as an entry point patient investors have been waiting for. Netflix shares are down 8.44% over the past week, 12.64% over the past month, and 44.1% over the past year, closing Tuesday at $68.67. That’s a sharp reset from levels near $95.55 at the start of April. Netflix’s Q2 2026 report on July 16 delivered EPS of $0.80 versus the $0.7883 estimate on revenue of $12.56 billion, up 13.37% year over year, with an operating margin of 33.4%. Growth was broad-based, with Latin America up 21%, Asia Pacific up 16%, EMEA up 14%, and North America up 10%. Netflix narrowly missed on Wall Street’s revenue expectations. However, Netflix fell from $73.985 at the filing to $68.20 within an hour. Cramer conceded the quarter was a disappointment with a weakening content slate, but pushed back on the idea that the company is now fundamentally broken. Cramer shared Netflix’s bull case based on an uptick in advertising revenue. “Advertising revenues should roughly double to $3 billion this year, and management believes the gap between the economics of the ad-supported and the ad-free plans is narrowing,” he said, pointing to a gap now under 45%. On the earnings call, co-CEO Gregory Peters described that closing gap as “near-term, unrealized revenue growth” the company can harvest. Then came the runway argument. “Penetration of its addressable broadband households captures only about 7% of the entertainment revenue available in those markets, and accounts for just about 5% of global television viewing, so there’s still plenty of room for growth,“ Cramer said. CFO Spencer Neumann noted Netflix is approaching 1 billion people in audience with household penetration under 45% of ~800 million addressable households. Additionally, Netflix repurchased $4.7 billion of stock in Q2, its largest buyback quarter ever, with roughly $27 billion in remaining authorization after April’s $25 billion refresh. Cramer characterized it as one of the largest corporate buyback programs in America, alongside investments across ads, games, live programming, podcasts, sports, and AI. Cramer advocated for interested investors to start a small position and scale up over time. “I’d put a small position here and then gradually add on to weakness in pyramid style, because I wouldn’t be surprised if the weakness sticks,” he said. Polymarket’s active weekly market assigns roughly 81% probability that NFLX closes the week of July 20-24 in the $60-$70 range, and the July monthly market puts the highest conviction at $65 with 0.405 probability. Reddit sentiment mirrors the split Cramer is trying to bridge. Aggregate sentiment scores dropped to 24 on Monday afternoon, while a widely upvoted r/stocks thread titled “Netflix beat earnings, did its biggest buyback and then restricted access to its engagement data and fell 12% through two days” captured the frustration. Wall Street’s average analyst price target sits at $97.91 with 37 Buy and 13 Hold ratings, which represents significant upside from the stock’s current price of $68.67. Cramer believes Netflix’s sell-off has created an attractive entry point, but he cautioned that the stock could remain weak in the near term. His strategy is to start with a small position and gradually buy more if shares continue to fall. The bull case now depends on Netflix doubling advertising revenue to $3 billion, restoring engagement growth, and meeting its Q3 guidance. If the company delivers, its global growth runway, record share repurchases, and 19x forward earnings multiple could make the current decline a long-term buying opportunity. Title: Turbulent Timeways is live with Classic Timewalking this week — and a frosty new mount for finishing four weeks
URL: https://blizzardwatch.com/2026/07/21/turbulent-timeways-back-12-0-7-dragonflight-dungeons-frosty-new-mount/
Time Published: 2026-07-21T21:00:20Z
Full Content:
NEXT | The Queue: Just a snuggly little guy Turbulent Timeways has returned during World of Warcraft’s patch 12.0.7, and is live through August 11. Turbulent Timeways is a favorite of mount collectors and leveling enthusiasts alike, in which we get multiple weeks of Timewalking dungeons in a row — perfect for building up a stockpile of Timewarped Badges — as well as access to an experience-increasing buff, Knowledge of the Timeways, that eventually culminates in a whopping 30% buff from Mastery of Timeways. Additionally, if you can achieve the Mastery of Timeways buff during any four weeks of the event (which is getting easier to do since the event runs for six weeks), you’ll get Master of the Turbulent Timeways V, which comes with a new mount: the frost-spiked Spawn of Vyranoth, modeled after the only one of the Primalists that actually likes us. Like usual, this is also an opportunity for Blizzard to introduce a new set of Timewalking dungeons, and as you might have guessed from the mount reward, this time around Dragonflight dungeons are joining the Timewalking lineup. Dragonflight Timewalking will both be the first week and the last week of the event, so make sure you note those dates: June 30 through August 11. The Dragonflight dungeon pool for Timewalking includes Brackenhide Hollow, The Azure Vaults, Ruby Life Pools, Halls of Infusion, Neltharus, and a dungeon that I think we’re all very familiar with at this point, Algeth’ar Academy. You’ll also find new Timewalking rewards available from Xydan, a new vendor in the Bronze Enclave in Valdrakken; highlights include the Liquid Hot Magma Slug mount, as well as a number of mounts that previously were rewards for Turbulent Timeways, like the Timely Buzzbee. There’s also the usual array of toys, pets, mounts, and starter gear available on all the Timewalking vendors. Also, if you’re more of a reputation hunter, you’ll also find reputation tokens for the relevant factions during their Timewalking week, along with a reputation buff for those factions — perfect for clearing up any Renowns or reputations you still want to max out in old content. Who knows, you might even get the lucky drop from any Timewalking dungeon of an Infinite Timereaver! If any of this strikes your fancy, here’s the full schedule of Timewalking in WoW patch 12.0.7: While the Black Temple raid is available during Burning Crusade week, it won’t have any additional rewards during Turbulent Timeways, nor does it count for your Mastery of the Turbulent Timeways. However, you can still earn 500 Timewarped Badges, and maybe this time you can hold onto Illidan’s glaives until he gets out. Keep telling yourself that. Remember, if you want to get Mastery of the Turbulent Timeways, you need to do any four Timewalking dungeons on the same character during any four weeks of the event to get (and keep) the Mastery of the Timeways buff. The weeks it don’t have to be consecutive, so if you really dislike those Classic Dungeons, you never need to see them. As a shortcut, you can do this achievement more easily by building up to Mastery of Timeways by running four Timewalking in one week on a character, logging off while they still have they buff, and then simply doing one dungeon in a subsequent week to maintain the buff — if you’re into the whole “minimum effort, maximum reward” thing! Originally published June 9, 2026. Most recently updated July 21, 2026. Blizzard Watch is a safe space for all readers. By leaving comments on this site you agree to follow our commenting and community guidelines. Toggle Dark Mode:

Title: NUPRC awards 37 oil blocks to 31 firms in 2026 licensing round
URL: https://www.vanguardngr.com/2026/07/nuprc-awards-37-oil-blocks-to-31-firms-in-2026-licensing-round/
Time Published: 2026-07-21T20:58:32Z
Full Content:
Vanguard News July 21, 2026 By Obas Esiedesa, Abuja The Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commission (NUPRC) has announced 31 companies as winners of 37 oil and gas blocks in the 2025 Licensing Round, following a competitive bidding process that attracted 200 bids from 143 companies. The winners emerged at the commercial bid conference held on Tuesday at the Transcorp Event Centre, Abuja, marking the conclusion of the competitive phase of the licensing exercise. According to the commission, the 37 awarded blocks were selected from the 50 oil and gas blocks offered across the Niger Delta onshore, shallow water and deep offshore areas, as well as the frontier basins of the Benue Trough, Chad Basin, Anambra Basin and Benin Basin. NUPRC said while 37 blocks attracted bids, 13 received no offers. It described the outcome as a milestone, noting that it was the first time Nigeria’s frontier basins had attracted such a high level of investor interest. The successful companies include SSonic Petroleum Limited, CFP Pipeline and Flowlines, Dutchford E&P Limited, Attabanson Global Company Limited, Rosem Energy Limited, Pivot-GIS Limited, Network E&P, Asharami, LexOil, BVOF, Gupsco Energy Limited, Saratoga, Volante, Concept-Reel Petroleum Services Limited, Clinton Oil Field and Nuway Oaklane Limited. Others are Ramec, Italia, Blueridge E&P, Up Energies Limited, AYM Shafa, Blackrock Holdings Limited, Funtay Integrated Business Limited, Riparian Development and Production Limited, Nikstallis, Stardeep Petroleum, Dakoda & U Limited, Southborne Oil and Gas Limited, Lanaka Petroleum, Highban Resources Limited and Eyre Energy Limited. Some companies secured more than one block. Attabanson Global Company Limited won PPL 2A33 and PPL 901, Gupsco Energy Limited secured PPL 2A44 and 2A51, Concept-Reel Petroleum Services Limited won PPL 2A47 and 2A55, Clinton Oil Field secured PPL 2A48 and 2A62, while Nikstallis emerged successful for PPL 2A61 and PPL 900. The commission, however, clarified that the companies would only receive their final awards after paying the required signature bonuses and obtaining the approval of the Minister of Petroleum Resources, in accordance with the Petroleum Industry Act, 2021. Speaking at the event, the Commission Chief Executive, Mrs. Oritsemeyiwa Eyesan, congratulated the successful bidders and urged them to promptly pay their signature bonuses and commence development of their assets. She warned that the commission would strictly enforce the “drill or drop” provisions of the Petroleum Industry Act, under which licence holders that fail to develop their assets within the stipulated timeframe risk losing them. Eyesan also expressed appreciation to President Bola Tinubu for supporting the commission in delivering what she described as a seamless licensing round. WhatsApp Channel Follow Vanguard Telegram Channel Follow Vanguard Comments expressed here do not reflect the opinions of Vanguard newspapers or any employee thereof. Title: 5 Semiconductor Stocks To Buy And 1 To Ignore
URL: https://www.forbes.com/sites/investor-hub/article/semiconductor-stocks-to-buy-and-ignore/
Time Published: 2026-07-21T20:53:39Z
Full Content:
ByJason Kirsch, Contributor. Edited bySophia Acevedo, Associate Editor. The semiconductor sector has performed well this year overall. AI infrastructure spending has been a significant tailwind for growth this year. However, not every chip stock deserves a spot in your portfolio, so it's important to favor companies with strong fundamentals and durable AI-driven growth. Top recommendations include NVIDIA, the undisputed AI chip leader; TSMC, the global foundry backbone; Micron, capitalizing on insatiable memory demand; Broadcom, dominant in custom AI accelerators; and Lam Research, a key supplier of wafer fabrication equipment. Despite recent stock surges, Intel may not be the as compelling of a choice. Investors should manage volatility through dollar-cost averaging and diversified portfolios across the AI supply chain, while closely monitoring valuations. The semiconductor sector is having a moment, even despite recent volatility. The benchmark Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ) is up roughly 100% year to date in 2026, easily lapping the broader tech sector. Behind the surge is a relentless wave of AI infrastructure spending from hyperscalers like Microsoft, Google and Meta, which are collectively expected to deploy nearly $700 billion in capital expenditures in 2026, with the majority earmarked for AI data centers. That's a massive tailwind for chipmakers across the supply chain. But not every chip stock deserves a spot in your portfolio. With valuations stretched across much of the sector, selectivity matters more than ever. I’ve identified five semiconductor stocks with compelling fundamentals and durable AI-driven growth catalysts — and one that investors may want to sidestep despite the hype surrounding it. Picking semiconductor stocks requires more than riding momentum. For the buy list, the focus was on companies with structural advantages: dominant market positions, improving earnings trajectories and direct exposure to the parts of the AI supply chain where demand is structurally supply-constrained rather than cyclically elevated. Revenue acceleration, analyst conviction and forward earnings estimates were all weighted heavily. AI spending is expected to stay strong in 2026, justifying premium valuations for the highest-quality companies. The stock to ignore was identified by the inverse criteria: where enthusiasm has outrun execution, where near-term profitability remains elusive despite a surging share price, and where the competitive position is more contested than the market currently prices in. Elevated valuations remain a common theme across much of the semiconductor industry, and distinguishing between AI beneficiaries and AI hype is the core task here. NVIDIA remains the undisputed engine of the AI chip revolution. In fiscal year 2026, NVIDIA’s revenue reached $215.9 billion — a 65% increase versus the prior year — with earnings growing at nearly the same pace. The company’s GPU architecture has become the default infrastructure for training and running AI models, cementing a competitive moat that rivals have struggled to breach. Despite the monster run, 48 analysts maintain a "Strong Buy" rating with a consensus price target implying roughly 46% upside from current levels. Nvidia estimates it can sell a trillion dollars' worth of data center AI chips in 2026 and 2027 combined — a number that, remarkably, looks achievable given current demand signals. The company has also identified a $200 billion AI CPU inference opportunity, expanding its total addressable market well beyond GPUs. With wafer-fabrication equipment spending accelerating and hyperscalers placing multi-year orders, Nvidia's revenue visibility is as strong as any company in tech. For investors with a multi-year horizon, NVDA remains the single clearest expression of the AI hardware megatrend. TSMC controls 72% of the global chip foundry market, making it the physical backbone of the AI supply chain. Every major AI chip — from NVIDIA’s GPUs to Broadcom’s custom ASICs — runs through TSMC’s fabs. Its dominance in advanced nodes including 3nm volume production and 2nm ramp-up gives it a near-irreplaceable strategic position. In 2026, TSMC has also raised its full-year revenue growth guidance. TSMC’s competitive moat is structural, not cyclical. Building a world-class semiconductor foundry takes years and tens of billions in capital investment — a barrier that makes TSMC’s position self-reinforcing. The adoption of agentic AI is expected to drive another wave of demand, as AI agents processing tasks without human interaction require significantly more token generation and compute capacity. Investors should monitor geopolitical risk given TSMC's Taiwan base, but for long-term holders, the company's combination of pricing power, technology leadership and AI-driven growth makes it one of the sector's most compelling holds. Micron is the memory play on AI infrastructure, and its numbers have turned extraordinary. Revenue spiked 196%, and non-GAAP earnings surged 682% year over year in its fiscal Q2 2026. The reason is structural: AI context lengths are increasing 30x per year while memory content per server has doubled in three years, creating an insatiable demand for DRAM and high-bandwidth memory (HBM) that Micron is uniquely positioned to supply. The company has already sold out its HBM supply for 2026, with significant 2027 capacity committed under long-term agreements. Morgan Stanley analysts had named Micron their top semiconductor pick for early 2026, arguing the ongoing AI buildout has created the most severe DRAM and NAND shortage in three decades. That shortage is structurally driving prices higher. With most major analysts in "buy" mode and memory demand showing no sign of softening, MU offers a compelling combination of growth and value within the chip sector. Broadcom has emerged as the dominant supplier of custom AI accelerators (ASICs), holding 70–80% market share in a segment that counts Alphabet’s Google, Meta, ByteDance, OpenAI and Anthropic among its customers. Unlike NVIDIA’s general-purpose GPUs, Broadcom's ASICs are purpose-built for each hyperscaler's specific AI workloads, offering performance and efficiency advantages at scale. Q2 revenues rose 47.9% year over year, with AI chip revenue alone reaching $10.8 billion in the quarter. The AI accelerator market is projected to expand at 29% annually through 2033, and Broadcom’s customer pipeline continues to grow. Beyond chips, Broadcom’s VMware acquisition adds a recurring software revenue stream in data center virtualization. That dual engine of custom AI chips and enterprise software makes AVGO one of the more diversified and durable names in the semiconductor space. Lam Research makes the machines that make the chips — specifically, deposition and etch equipment used by foundries and memory manufacturers. It’s a pick-and-shovel play on the entire AI semiconductor build-out. Lam raised its 2026 wafer fabrication equipment (WFE) spending forecast to $140 billion, and third-party estimates from Counterpoint Research put the number even higher at $159 billion. Whether chips are made for AI training, inference or memory, Lam's equipment is almost always in the manufacturing chain. The beauty of Lam’s position is that it benefits regardless of which AI chip design wins. Consensus estimates project a 26% revenue increase in FY2026 followed by a 31% jump in FY2027, and Bank of America projects the global semiconductor market could clock a 28% CAGR through 2030, topping $2 trillion in annual revenue. That sustained growth cycle translates directly into sustained WFE demand. For investors who want AI chip exposure with less concentration risk in any single chip designer, LRCX offers an elegant upstream alternative. Intel’s stock has surged on AI optimism, powered by government investment, new foundry contracts and a narrative of turnaround. But the underlying financials tell a more complicated story. Intel's chip manufacturing segment posted a $2.4 billion operating loss in Q1 2026, despite $5.4 billion in sales. The foundry business remains deeply unprofitable, and while deals with Apple, Microsoft and SpaceX are promising, they have not yet yielded meaningful financial gains. Morningstar does not believe Intel has an economic moat. Morningstar has argued that its manufacturing setbacks have eliminated competitive advantages that chip design expertise alone cannot restore. Intel’s returns on invested capital have fallen in recent years, and Morningstar foresees no excess returns on capital in the years ahead. Until the foundry business turns profitable and Intel demonstrates sustained execution, the risk/reward doesn't justify the current valuation. Even the best semiconductor stocks can be volatile, and 2026 has offered a clear reminder of that. For example, in early June, AMD and Intel shares dropped more than 10% in a single session following cautious AI chip guidance from Broadcom — despite the broader AI thesis remaining intact. For investors, the practical lesson is that single-session drawdowns in high-growth sectors are common. Dollar-cost averaging — investing a fixed amount at regular intervals regardless of price — is one of the most effective tools for managing this kind of volatility without trying to time the market. Portfolio construction matters too. The AI semiconductor opportunity extends well beyond a handful of mega-cap names, with critical roles played across memory, connectivity, optical interconnects, power efficiency and compute infrastructure. Spreading exposure across the supply chain — GPU designers, foundries, memory makers and equipment suppliers — reduces concentration risk in any single company or earnings cycle. A position in LRCX, for example, is a very different bet than a position in NVDA, even though both benefit from the same underlying trend. Finally, keep an eye on valuation anchors. Semiconductor stocks are inherently cyclical, and even structurally strong companies can trade well ahead of their fundamentals during a momentum-driven rally. Checking forward P/E ratios, revenue growth rates and analyst price targets regularly — and being willing to trim positions that have run far ahead of fair value — is a disciplined approach that protects long-term gains. Volatility in a secular growth sector is most dangerous when it catches investors with undiversified, over-leveraged positions. The AI chip cycle is real, the demand is structural, and some stocks offer distinct, high-quality entry points across the semiconductor supply chain. Intel, despite its dramatic share price recovery, remains a turnaround story with execution risk and a foundry business that's still burning cash. In a sector this hot, knowing what to avoid is just as important as knowing what to buy. What Makes a Semiconductor Stock A Strong "Buy"? A strong semiconductor buy combines durable competitive positioning with clear, near-term earnings acceleration. Specifically, the best picks tend to dominate a scarce or supply-constrained layer of the AI chip supply chain, have improving analyst consensus, and trade at valuations supported by credible multi-year growth projections. Companies with direct exposure to AI infrastructure spending — where hyperscaler capex has already been committed — offer the most visibility into sustained demand. Why Should Certain Tech Or Chip Stocks Be Ignored Despite High Market Interest? High market interest alone is not a fundamental reason to buy a stock — and in semiconductors, it can be a warning sign. Elevated valuations remain a common theme across much of the semiconductor industry after the sector's AI-driven rally, and stocks that have surged on narrative rather than earnings tend to punish latecomers most severely. The key red flags to watch for: unprofitable core segments, no sustainable competitive moat, and a share price that already prices in a flawless execution scenario that the company has yet to demonstrate. How Does Cyclicality Impact High-Growth AI Semiconductor Stocks? Cyclicality is baked into the semiconductor industry's DNA, and the AI boom doesn't eliminate it — it just shifts the cycle's timing. Even during the current bull run, inventory corrections, memory supply imbalances, and demand shifts between consumer electronics and data centers can create sharp swings in revenue and stock prices. The best strategy is to focus on companies with structural AI tailwinds rather than cyclically elevated demand, and to size positions with enough patience to ride out the inevitable downturns that come with owning high-growth chip stocks.
--------------------------------------------------

Title: 5 Semiconductor Stocks To Buy And 1 To Ignore
URL: https://www.forbes.com/sites/investor-hub/article/semiconductor-stocks-to-buy-and-ignore/
Time Published: 2026-07-21T20:53:39Z
Full Content:
ByJason Kirsch, Contributor. Edited bySophia Acevedo, Associate Editor. The semiconductor sector has performed well this year overall.
